author: Aubert, Bernard and others
title: Measurement of the Semileptonic Decays $B\\to D\\tau^{-}\\bar{\\nu}_{\\tau}$ and $B\\to D^{*}\\tau^{-}\\bar{\\nu}_{\\tau}$
abstract: We present measurements of the semileptonic decays $B^{-}\\to D^{0}\\tau^{-}\\bar{\\nu}_{\\tau}$, $B^{-}\\to D^{*0}\\tau^{-}\\bar{\\nu}_{\\tau}$, $\\bar{B}^{0}\\to D^{+}\\tau^{-}\\bar{\\nu}_{\\tau}$, and $\\bar{B}^{0}\\to D^{*+}\\tau^{-}\\bar{\\nu}_{\\tau}$, which are sensitive to non--Standard Model amplitudes. The measurements are based on a data sample of $383\\times 10^{6}$ $B\\bar{B}$ pairs recorded by the BABAR detector. The $\\tau^{-}$ is identified in decay modes with a single charged hadron plus neutrals, or with three charged hadrons and a $\\pi^{0}$. The signal $B$ meson is fully reconstructed in a hadronic decay mode, while the rest of the event is used to identify the $\\tau$. We measure the branching fractions $\\mathcal{B}(B\\to D\\tau\\nu)=(0.86\\pm 0.24\\pm 0.12)\\%$ and $\\mathcal{B}(B\\to D^{*}\\tau\\nu)=(1.62\\pm 0.31\\pm 0.10)\\%$, and the ratios $R(D)\\equiv\\mathcal{B}(B\\to D\\tau\\nu)/\\mathcal{B}(B\\to D\\ell\\nu)=0.416\\pm 0.117\\pm 0.055$ and $R(D^{*})\\equiv\\mathcal{B}(B\\to D^{*}\\tau\\nu)/\\mathcal{B}(B\\to D^{*}\\ell\\nu)=0.297\\pm 0.056\\pm 0.026$, where $\\ell$ refers to either an electron or a muon. The results are in agreement with the Standard Model expectations.
